questions.question: What is reflection of light?
questions.answer: Reflection of light is the phenomenon in which light bounces back into the same medium after striking a surface.

questions.question: State the two laws of reflection.
questions.answer: 1. The angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection. 2. The incident ray, reflected ray, and normal lie in the same plane.

questions.question: What is a plane mirror?
questions.answer: A plane mirror is a flat reflecting surface that forms virtual, erect, and laterally inverted images of the same size as the object.

questions.question: What are spherical mirrors?
questions.answer: Spherical mirrors are curved mirrors whose reflecting surface is part of a sphere, either concave or convex.

questions.question: Define the principal focus of a concave mirror.
questions.answer: The principal focus of a concave mirror is the point on the principal axis where parallel rays converge after reflection.

questions.question: What is the focal length of a spherical mirror?
questions.answer: Focal length is half the radius of curvature (f = R/2).

questions.question: Give one use of a concave mirror.
questions.answer: Concave mirrors are used in torches, searchlights, and solar furnaces to concentrate light rays.

questions.question: Give one use of a convex mirror.
questions.answer: Convex mirrors are used as rear-view mirrors in vehicles because they give an erect and wider field of view.

questions.question: What is refraction of light?
questions.answer: Refraction of light is the bending of light when it passes from one transparent medium to another due to change in speed.

questions.question: State Snell's law of refraction.
questions.answer: The ratio of sine of angle of incidence to sine of angle of refraction is constant for a given pair of media (sin i / sin r = n).

questions.question: What is refractive index?
questions.answer: Refractive index of a medium is the ratio of speed of light in vacuum to speed of light in that medium (n = c/v).

questions.question: Why does a pencil appear bent in water?
questions.answer: Due to refraction, light from the immersed part bends at the water-air interface, making the pencil appear bent.

questions.question: What is lateral displacement in a glass slab?
questions.answer: Lateral displacement is the sideways shift of the emergent ray parallel to the incident ray when light passes through a glass slab.

questions.question: What is a convex lens?
questions.answer: A convex lens is thicker at the centre and thinner at the edges, converging light rays to a point.

questions.question: What is a concave lens?
questions.answer: A concave lens is thinner at the centre and thicker at the edges, diverging light rays.

questions.question: Define the power of a lens.
questions.answer: Power of a lens is the ability to converge or diverge light rays, measured as the reciprocal of focal length in metres (P = 1/f).

questions.question: What is the sign convention for mirrors?
questions.answer: Object distance (u) is negative, real image and focal length for concave mirror are negative, virtual image positive.

questions.question: Why do convex mirrors form virtual images?
questions.answer: Convex mirrors diverge light rays, so they appear to come from behind the mirror, forming virtual, erect, and diminished images.

questions.question: Why is the refractive index of glass greater than that of water?
questions.answer: Light travels slower in glass than in water, so glass has higher optical density and higher refractive index.

questions.question: What happens when light goes from denser to rarer medium?
questions.answer: Light bends away from the normal, and if angle of incidence exceeds critical angle, total internal reflection occurs.

questions.question: Why do stars twinkle?
questions.answer: Twinkling occurs due to refraction of starlight through Earth's atmosphere with varying density layers, causing apparent position changes.

questions.question: What is the lens formula?
questions.answer: The lens formula is 1/f = 1/v - 1/u, where f is focal length, v is image distance, u is object distance.

questions.question: Define magnification for lenses.
questions.answer: Magnification (m) is the ratio of image height to object height, also m = v/u (with signs).

questions.question: Why are convex lenses used in magnifying glasses?
questions.answer: Convex lenses form virtual, erect, and enlarged images when the object is placed between the lens and focal point.

questions.question: What is the unit of power of a lens?
questions.answer: The SI unit of power of a lens is dioptre (D), where 1 D = 1 m⁻¹.
